meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: World leaders condemn ‘unacceptable’ violence after armed attack disrupts WH Correspondents’ Dinner
items.summary: Global leaders expressed relief that Trump was unharmed after an armed suspect allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington.

items.title: Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'
items.summary: Trump canceled planned Iran talks in Pakistan, citing regime infighting, and experts say Tehran's good cop, bad cop negotiating tactic has collapsed.

items.title: Latin American leftists met in Spain, signaling push against US influence on continent
items.summary: Analysts say the Barcelona summit led by Spain's Pedro Sánchez signals a progressive bloc forming to challenge U.S. influence in Latin America and beyond.

items.title: US embassy in London warns to 'exercise increased caution' near Jewish sites in UK, Europe after attacks
items.summary: Americans in the U.K. and Europe are urged to exercise caution after the U.S. Embassy in London cited rising threats to Jewish and American areas.

items.title: Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire
items.summary: Hamas reportedly increased its hold in Gaza as Deir al-Balah holds its first elections in 20 years, raising concerns about terrorist group influence.

items.title: US targets Chinese refinery in sweeping Iran oil crackdown, sanctions ‘shadow fleet’ tankers
items.summary: The OFAC sanctioned a major Chinese oil refinery and dozens of Iran-linked shadow fleet vessels in an escalation of a maximum pressure campaign on Tehran.

items.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
items.summary: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

items.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
items.summary: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.

items.title: Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm
items.summary: Experts say the U.S. must permanently ban Iran's uranium enrichment program, warning Tehran could resume its nuclear weapons path if infrastructure stays.

items.title: Ships seized by Iranians 'armed to the teeth' along Strait of Hormuz have been taken toward port: report
items.summary: The two container ships seized by Iran this week in the Strait of Hormuz have been moved toward the port of Bandar Abbas along the key waterway, a report said.

items.title: 2 trains collide in Denmark, leaving 5 people critically injured
items.summary: Two trains collided near Copenhagen, Denmark, early Thursday, leaving five critically injured and roughly a dozen others with minor injuries, police say.

items.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
items.summary: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

items.title: Hezbollah disarmament deadlock risks civil war, analysts say, as US prepares for Israel–Lebanon talks
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio hosts Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington as diplomats confront the unresolved question of Hezbollah's disarmament.

items.title: Former Mexican beauty queen found shot dead as investigators examine possible family involvement: reports
items.summary: Prosecutors investigate the death of Carolina Flores Gómez, a former Mexican beauty queen found shot in her Mexico City apartment in the Polanco area.

items.title: Landlords allegedly posting 'Muslim-only' apartment ads in violation of country's equality act: report
items.summary: Some London landlords are reportedly advertising 'Muslim-only' rental listings on Facebook and other platforms, allegedly violating Britain's Equality Act.

items.title: Iran agrees not to execute eight women tied to anti-regime protests after Trump's public appeal
items.summary: President Donald Trump says eight women protesters will no longer be executed in Iran after he urged their release during ongoing negotiations.

items.title: Freed hostage Rom Braslavski details abuse, starvation during 738 days in Gaza captivity
items.summary: Rom Braslavski, abducted from the Supernova festival Oct. 7, describes brutal abuse, starvation and sexual violence during his captivity in Gaza.

items.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
items.summary: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.

items.title: Voice for kids: 11-year-old Israeli boy uses social media to battle antisemitism
items.summary: Ben Carasso, Israel's 11-year-old "Spokeskid," advocates for children affected by conflict, speaking out despite facing antisemitic abuse online.

items.title: Iran reportedly fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The Islamic Republic of Iran reportedly opened fire on three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday amid ongoing tensions in the region.

items.title: Tokyo theme park worker dies after being trapped inside ride mechanism during routine maintenance
items.summary: An employee conducting routine maintenance at Tokyo Dome City Attractions reportedly died after becoming trapped in the "Flying Balloon" ride mechanism.

items.title: Pope Leo urges Africans to stay and 'serve your country' instead of migrating as displacement climbs
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV urged African youth to stay and improve their own countries rather than migrate while speaking to university students in Cameroon's capital.

items.title: Israel unveils game-changing artillery against Iran-backed Hezbollah amid fragile ceasefire
items.summary: Israel deploys its new self-propelled Ro'em howitzer against Hezbollah targets as ceasefire talks between Israel and Lebanon slated to continue in Washington later this week.

items.title: Mexico temple gunman ranted about pyramid's gruesome history to hostages: ‘Don’t move, or I’ll sacrifice you’
items.summary: Video shows the Teotihuacan pyramids gunman reportedly bragging about sacrificing victims before killing a Canadian tourist and wounding others.

items.title: UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?
items.summary: Britain's elite SAS faces a growing exodus as soldiers resign over fears of legal scrutiny for actions in Afghanistan and other conflicts, report says.
